title: entada woodii
intro: Entada woodii is a plant species commonly known as the "elephant ear" or "bead tree," belonging to the Legume family and native to tropical Africa. The plant's seeds, also known as beads, have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The seeds have been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and have been shown to have health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Entada woodii seeds can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are also used in traditional African rituals and ceremonies.
